name: Cooper-Harper Rating
summary: A pilot-centred handling-qualities scale used in flight test to judge whether an aircraft is easy, acceptable, or unacceptable to fly for a given task.
whenToUseIt: Use it during handling-qualities assessment when pilot feedback needs to be captured in a repeatable way rather than as vague opinion.
steps: Define the flight task and pilot workload condition.
Fly or simulate the task in a controlled configuration.
Ask whether the pilot can achieve the task performance required.
Rate the aircraft using the Cooper-Harper decision tree.
Compare ratings across configurations to guide control-law or handling improvements.
example: Two control-law tunings may both be stable, but one demands much more pilot compensation during landing flare. Cooper-Harper ratings help the team quantify that difference and justify design changes.
